hero_title: Structured AML Controls for VARA Compliance
hero_subtitle: SFTY helps digital asset compliance teams manage jurisdiction-specific VARA AML rules by connecting provider outputs into audit-ready workflows with case management and regulatory rule monitoring.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling

trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance workflows stall across jurisdictional gaps
problem_description: VARA-facing compliance teams struggle to align AML controls with evolving local regulations and requirements. This leads to inconsistent rule application, fragmented audit evidence, and delays in case management and reporting workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific AML rules, provider outputs, alert triage and case management into one operational workflow that supports consistent VARA compliance and clear audit-ready evidence packages.
workflow_title: From regulatory updates to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY monitors jurisdiction-specific regulatory changes and maps them into updated internal policies, workflows, case creation and audit-ready evidence for VARA AML compliance.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VARA compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that structures VARA AML rules, provider outputs, cases, evidence and reports into one operational workflow for VASPs under VARA regulations.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support VARA AML workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects onboarding, KYT, Travel Rule, screening and regulatory updates into jurisdiction-specific VARA AML workflows with case management and decision logging for clearer compliance operations.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve VARA audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence by recording case histories, decision logs, provider results and regulatory change links to support review and inspection readiness under VARA requirements.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing AML providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates with your existing KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers to connect outputs into governed workflows without disrupting current AML vendor relationships.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace our VARA compliance tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace existing providers but connects them into one structured compliance layer supporting VARA AML controls, improving workflow efficiency and audit traceability.
final_title: Structure VARA AML controls into audit workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects provider outputs to support VARA AML case management, regulatory monitoring and audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
